Off Calendar Dec-29-2020 Case Transferred

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Transfer Or Proceedings To Contra Costa County
Off Calendar Dec-22-2020 Continued To Feb-02-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Establish Special Needs Trust; To Appoint Trustee, To Set Trustee'S Bond; Authorize Inte
Off Calendar Nov-10-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Kevin Urbatsch appeared by zoom for Chance Froman.

Mr. Urbatsch explained why he questions the need for a budget stating that they don't have anyone with legal authority to create a budget, that any information provided would be a complete guess and that he was surprised to see the local rule. The Court explained that the corpus of the special needs trust can't be more than the needs and that it needs to make an analysis as to whether it's too much. The Court also spoke of its time of serving in Dept. 514 and stated that its memory is that the special needs trust is to be established in the County in which it is going to be administered, and then there was a settlement. Mr. Urbatsch stated that pursuant to the code it is supposed to be heard in the same department, that Counsel is having a problem getting the settlement approved until this is decided and that it is his understanding that special needs trusts are to be heard in Probate. The Court questions the amount requested for purchase of a home in Santa Cruz. Mr. Urbatsch stated that the request is up to $2,000,000.00, that it is about 20% of the overall value of the proceeds of the trust, and that buying property in California is investing the money in a home for Lillian. The Court questions why not ask Contra Costa County. Mr. Urbatsch stated that the plan to move has changed, that initially it was Santa Cruz, that Lillian can't fly, that she can only travel by automobile, and that he believes the family has been in Colorado, so they are looking for the ability to move. Mr. Urbatsch also spoke of Lillian's medical condition, why she is unable to travel and why a Winnebago is needed, stated that they estimated the amount a little higher in case there are any unforeseen expenses and that the proposed Trustee is a very experienced Fiduciary in California. The Court stated that it wants to limit Contra Costa County's discretion as little as possible. The Petition to Establish Special Needs Trust, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
